definition: The lawful conditionality by which, when this is, that comes to be.
notes: This term names the broader principle of conditionality expressed in formulas such as 'when this is, this comes to be.' Buddhadasa Bhikkhu treats idappaccayata as a foundational key to understanding dependent arising as a natural law of lived experience, and the repository records that emphasis here. Conditionality is the cleanest default, while this-conditionality remains available when the morphology needs to be made explicit.

context_rules.context: In explanatory prose focused on the morphology of the term
context_rules.notes: Use when the idiom of this conditioned by this is central.

context_rules.context: In Buddhadasa-style explanation of natural law
context_rules.notes: Notes may explicitly describe it as the natural law behind dependent arising.

example_phrases.translation: when this is, this comes to be
